mnemonics.title: Leaf Finding Technique
mnemonics.content: To find centers: REMOVE LEAVES repeatedly - Remaining 1 or 2 vertices are CENTERS

mnemonics.title: Radius-Diameter Relation
mnemonics.content: RADii (R) is always half or more of DIAMeter (D): R ≤ D ≤ 2R

mnemonics.title: Tree Properties
mnemonics.content: TREE = Connected + Acyclic = n vertices → n-1 edges

mnemonics.title: Eccentricity Meaning
mnemonics.content: ECCENTRIC = EXTREME - Eccentricity is the MAX distance to any vertex

acronyms.expansion: Pendant (leaf), End, Degree - leaf has degree 1
acronyms.usage: Remember pendant vertices have degree 1 and are endpoints of paths
Distance defines eccentricity, which defines center

acronyms.expansion: Radius And Diameter Definition - R=min ecc, D=max ecc
acronyms.usage: Radius is minimum eccentricity, Diameter is maximum distance

visualTips.visualization: Draw tree, circle all leaves, remove them in one step. Repeat until 1-2 vertices remain. Those are centers.
Think of tree as road network - distance is shortest road length using unique path between two locations.
Imagine standing at a vertex, find farthest vertex - that distance is eccentricity. Center is where farthest point is closest.
Central hub with spokes - the hub is clearly the center since all leaves are distance 1 away.

analogies.analogy: Like finding the best location for a hospital - minimize maximum distance to any neighborhood. The center is optimal location.
Think of eccentricity like the reach of a radio tower - how far its signal can reach from that point.
Tree is like a family tree without marriages - unique ancestor-descendant path, no loops.
Like a dead-end street - the street ends at the leaf, only one way in and out.
